title: Garlicky Creamy Smoky Kenyan Fennel Stew
description: A rich and flavorful stew from Kenya featuring fennel seeds and a smoky heat without onions or garlic.

ingredients.name: fennel bulbs
ingredients.notes: 1 large or 2 small fennel bulbs cut into 1-inch pieces

ingredients.name: coconut oil
ingredients.notes: or other neutral oil for saut5ing

ingredients.name: chicken breast meat
ingredients.notes: bony parts removed, cut into bite-sized pieces

ingredients.name: kenyan coriander spice mix
ingredients.notes: available at African markets or online

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: or chicken broth for added depth

instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a clay pot over medium heat.
Add the chicken and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the fennel and cook for 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the Kenyan coriander spice mix, hot smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Pour in the water and bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low and cook, covered, for 15-20 minutes, or until the chicken and fennel are tender.
Stir in the plain yogurt and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until heated through.
Serve hot, garnished with additional fennel seeds if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
